ops.title: Fan-out scratch directories must be created via --mint-root, never hand-picked
ops.body:
# Fan-out scratch: codex-ask --mint-root is the only mint

Since codex plugin 0.18.0 (commit 8c76d761), `codex-ask --mint-root [LANE ...]` is the sole sanctioned way to create a codex fan-out root and lane roster. Sessions must never hand-pick a scratch location (`.claude/scratch/`, `~/.claude/*`, repo-relative paths, ad-hoc mktemp recipes) — that habit produced in-repo lane dirs (version-control pollution, worktree confusion) and junk under `~/.claude` (a config dir, not scratch space).

## What --mint-root guarantees

- **Placement**: the root resolves exactly like a plain no-`-s` ask — the harness session scratchpad when `cc-transcript scratchpad` resolves one, else `mktemp -d "$TMPDIR/codex-root.XXXXXX"`. Never in-repo, never under `~/.claude`.
- **All-or-nothing roster**: lane dirs are created before anything prints; a mid-roster failure removes the minted root and emits zero output, so the documented consume pattern (`ROOT=$(codex-ask --mint-root <lanes...> | sed -n 's/^ROOT: //p')`, guarded with `[ -n "$ROOT" ]`) can never capture a partial mint.
- **Lane-name constraints**: empty, leading `.` or `-`, embedded `/` or newline are rejected (dot-lanes are invisible to `--collect`'s glob; newlines forge protocol lines); duplicate detection is case-insensitive (case-insensitive filesystems collapse `review`/`Review` into one dir, hitting the last-writer-wins meta race).
- **Protocol lines** are emitted with `printf` (immune to inherited `xpg_echo`).

## Enforcement at the boundaries

- `-s` rejects paths under `~/.claude` (before any mkdir) in addition to the pre-existing absolute-only and outside-the-current-repo checks.
- `--collect` rejects in-repo and `~/.claude` roots before its existence check, so a planned-but-never-minted bad root gets the policy error, not "no such directory".

## Accepted, deliberate gaps (do not "fix" without a decision)

- Path matching is literal, not canonicalized: `..`-spelled paths can sidestep or over-trigger the checks. Foot-gun guard, not a security boundary — matches the pre-existing repo check's style.
- The repo check derives the repository from the caller's cwd (`git rev-parse`), so `-s` into a *different* repo from outside it passes.
- An operator-exported in-repo absolute `$TMPDIR` defeats placement; pre-existing hazard shared with the plain ask path.

Guidance lives in `plugins/codex/skills/codex/SKILL.md` (fan-out step 1) and the fleet-propagated `plugin/guides/md/claude-rules.md` fan-out paragraph — both name `--mint-root` and forbid hand-minting.
ops.when: Modifying scratch directory handling, fan-out orchestration, or codex-ask command interfaces
